title: HBR registry loader + applicability evaluator (Rust + Node)
goal: Land the canonical HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json loader and applicability predicate evaluator in both Rust (for runtime handoff-gate use) and Node (for build-time gov-check use). This is the foundational primitive every subsequent A.1 MT depends on: a single, schema-validated, version-checked reader of the 23 active HBR rules with a deterministic applicability evaluator over packet metadata + touched-path globs.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/hbr/registry.rs + src/backend/handshake_core/src/hbr/applicability.rs + mod.rs entry; new Node module .GOV/roles_shared/scripts/hbr-registry-loader.mjs; unit tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/hbr_registry_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: No prerequisites; this is the foundation MT for A.1.
implementation_notes: Per spec §5.6.1 (HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES authority) the file at .GOV/roles_shared/records/HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json is the single source-of-truth (AC-HBR-AUTHORITY). The Rust side: create a public `HbrRegistry` struct with `load_from_path(p: &Path) -> Result<HbrRegistry, HbrError>` that parses the JSON via serde_json, validates `schema == "handshake.build_rules@1"`, `name == "HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES"`, and `version` matches a SemVer pattern. Each rule must deserialize into an `HbrRule { id, pillar (enum INT|SWARM|VIS|QUIET|MAN), status, novelty, trigger: String, applicability: HbrApplicability { predicate_text, tags: Vec<String> }, required_action, evidence_kind, citations: Vec<HbrCitation>, not_applicable_when: Vec<String>, deferred_until: Option<String> }`. Expose `registry.active_rules() -> impl Iterator<Item=&HbrRule>` filtering on status=="ACTIVE". The applicability evaluator: `HbrApplicability::evaluate(rule: &HbrRule, ctx: &PacketContext) -> Applicability { Applicable, NotApplicable { reason: String } }` where `PacketContext` holds `wp_id`, `touched_paths: Vec<PathBuf>`, `tags_declared: Vec<String>`, and `not_applicable_overrides: Vec<HbrNaOverride { hbr_id, reason }>`. Predicate semantics: a rule is Applicable if any of its `applicability.tags` matches a tag declared by the packet OR any touched path matches a glob known to that tag (define a starter tag->glob mapping table inline: e.g., `model_invocation` matches `**/model_runtime/**`, `crdt` matches `**/kernel/crdt/**`, `process_lifecycle` matches `**/process_ledger/**`, `automation_surface` matches `app/src-tauri/**` and `tests/visual/**`). The Node side: hbr-registry-loader.mjs exports `loadRegistry(path)` and `evaluateApplicability(rule, ctx)` mirroring the Rust semantics so gov-check sub-checks (MT-003) and validator-scan integration (MT-005) can reuse identical logic. Cargo.toml additions: serde, serde_json, thiserror, glob (or globset). Tests: parse the live HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json, assert 23 active rules, assert pillar distribution (INT:8, SWARM:4, VIS:5, QUIET:4, MAN:3), assert that HBR-INT-001 is Applicable for a context with `tags_declared=["observable_behavior"]`, and assert that NotApplicable reasons are non-empty when `not_applicable_overrides` matches.
risk_if_missed: Without a canonical registry loader every downstream A.1 MT reinvents parsing and applicability semantics inconsistently; PACKET_ACCEPTANCE_MATRIX hydration becomes silently wrong.
validator_focus: Rust and Node implementations agree byte-for-byte on (rule_id, applicability) pairs for a fixture context; schema validation rejects a tampered registry; iteration is deterministic.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Schema validation rejects any registry file whose schema/name/version fields do not match the v1.1.0 contract.
Applicability evaluator is pure (no I/O) so unit tests deterministically cover all 23 rules.
Rust and Node loaders share a golden-fixture cross-check test to prevent semantic drift between build-time and runtime evaluation.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Rust + Node loaders implement schema/applicability correctly and a tampering-rejection test exists, but the Node self-test asserts 24 active rules and a pillar distribution that excludes STOP entirely — it will fail against the live v1.3.0 registry (29 active rules across INT/SWARM/VIS/QUIET/MAN/STOP).

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: Rust + Node loaders implement schema/applicability correctly and a tampering-rejection test exists, but the Node self-test asserts 24 active rules and a pillar distribution that excludes STOP entirely — it will fail against the live v1.3.0 registry (29 active rules across INT/SWARM/VIS/QUIET/MAN/STOP).
